title: quercus × substellata
intro:
Quercus × substellata, also known as the Subelliptica oak or Intermedia oak, is a hybrid plant of the Quercus robur and Quercus coccinea species, characterized by its distinctive elliptical-shaped leaves.

This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant qualities, which are derived from its high tannin and polyphenol content.

The health benefits of Quercus × substellata include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to prevent certain types of cancer and protect against oxidative stress.

Quercus × substellata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
